 Research Engine Round 1 — Surgical Wins, Feature Flags, Real Measurement

 Session: 60

 Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/spicy-sleeping-gadget.md

 Last rebuilt: 2026-04-13 (after pressure-test pass)

 ---

 Context

 Scout's research engine ( tools/research_engine.py , 1,795 lines, 20-layer pipeline) is Steven's bottleneck for bulk prospecting work once Scout is actually in active use. Today's economics don't fit Steven's

 real constraints and have to change before the pipeline can scale.

 Measured baseline (N=20 public district jobs, real Research Log data, parsed 2026-04-13):

 - Wall clock: mean 6-7 min/job, serial queue (one job at a time)

 - Serper queries: ~57/job typical

 - Total contacts: median 43, mean 43.7, range 0-90

 - Verified emails: median 5, mean 21.9 (huge skew — some jobs get 0-5, some get 80+)

 - Per-job cost: $0.35-0.95/job (estimate — not yet measured with real token counts)

 Steven's real constraints (Session 60 conversation):

 - Budget: $25/week hard ceiling. Lower is better.

 - Email cap: Outreach enforces 5,000 emails/rolling-7-days per user, meaning ~100 research jobs/week at saturation at current yield profile. Steven is not at saturation today.

 - Quality bar: no sacrifice. Every optimization must preserve or improve yield vs current behavior.

 - Speed: negotiable — batch and overnight scheduling are acceptable for bulk work. Interactive single-target speed is nice-to-have, not a must.

 - Primary lane: public school districts in 13 territory states (TX, CA-SoCal, IL, PA, OH, MI, CT, OK, MA, IN, NV, TN, NE). Diocesan, charter CMOs, CTE centers, private school networks are SECONDARY scanners

 — they do not lead design, examples, or test targets.

 - Preserve the old engine byte-for-byte so it can serve as A/B control. Do not retire it, do not replace it.

 Honest framing of what Round 1 does and doesn't do:

 Round 1 is NOT the endgame. It does NOT hit the $25/week budget by itself. Round 1's job is:

 1. Fix two silent quality bugs that are losing VERIFIED emails today in BOTH the current engine and any future engine

 2. Ship the cheapest, lowest-risk cost wins that don't require new infrastructure

 3. Build the A/B measurement harness that future rounds will use to validate their wins

 4. Instrument real Claude token usage logging so ALL future cost analysis is measurement-grade, not estimation-grade

 5. Preserve current engine behavior as the default (feature-flag gated) so production risk is near zero

 6. Establish the numerical ship/no-ship gates that Round 2 and Round 3 must pass

 The real cost-reduction levers (entity cache, Claude call batching, model swap, job-level parallelism) live in Rounds 2 and 3. Round 1 is the foundation. It needs to be right so the rounds on top of it

 don't need to be redone.

 ---

 Measurement (the real data this plan is anchored to)

 Pulled from the Research Log tab of Steven's main Google Sheet, N=20 public district jobs (diocesan/charter/CTE/private-network filtered out). Per-layer yield parsed from the notes field column H.

 Per-layer contact yield aggregates ( measurement ):

 ┌─────────────────────┬──────────┬───────────────────────────┬────────┬───────┬─────┐

 │ Layer │ Hit rate │ Mean yield (when present) │ Median │ Total │ Max │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L1 direct-title │ 75% │ 10.4 │ 9 │ 156 │ 24 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L2 title-variations │ 90% │ 12.6 │ 14 │ 227 │ 30 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L3 linkedin │ 75% │ 7.7 │ 7 │ 115 │ 14 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L4 district-site │ 35% │ 1.4 │ 1 │ 10 │ 3 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L5 news-grants │ 25% │ 1.6 │ 1 │ 8 │ 3 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L11 school-staff │ 20% │ 2.0 │ 2 │ 8 │ 4 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L12 board-agendas │ 20% │ 1.0 │ 1 │ 4 │ 1 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L13 state-doe │ 15% │ 5.0 │ 5 │ 15 │ 9 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L14 conference │ 70% │ 3.8 │ 3 │ 53 │ 7 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L15 email-verify │ 40% │ 6.0 │ 5 │ 48 │ 13 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L16 exa-broad │ 25% │ 28.6 │ 25 │ 143 │ 58 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L17 exa-domain │ 20% │ 9.5 │ 15 │ 38 │ 17 │

 ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼───────────────────────────┼────────┼───────┼─────┤

 │ L20 brave │ 10% │ 3.0 │ 5 │ 6 │ 5 │

 └─────────────────────┴──────────┴───────────────────────────┴────────┴───────┴─────┘

 What the data tells us:

 - L1+L2+L3 are the reliable core. 75-90% hit rate, 498 of ~900 total contacts observed come from these three layers alone. Cutting them would gut yield.

 - L2 is the single biggest workhorse. 90% hit rate, mean 12.6 contacts when it hits, max 30 on good runs. Highest-confidence layer in the pipeline.

 - L14 (conference presenter) is quietly reliable. 70% hit rate. Stays in the core.

 - L16 (Exa broad) is the variance bomb. Only 25% hit rate, but mean 28.6 when it hits and max 58. When it works it dominates the run; when it doesn't, it adds cost without yield.

 - L16/L17 indirectly amplify L9 Claude cost. Each Exa result becomes a ~15k-char page appended to raw_pages , which then flows through L9's Claude Sonnet 4.6 extraction at an estimated ~$0.04/page. So

 L16+L17 firing ~4 Exa queries also generates 10-30 extra Claude calls downstream — the indirect L9 cost (~$0.40-1.20/job when Exa hits) dwarfs the direct Exa API cost (~$0.04).

 - L4/L5/L11/L12/L13/L20 are low-yield candidates. 10-35% hit rate, mean 1-5 contacts each. Candidates for conditional execution in later rounds. L4 must stay regardless because it's the domain-discovery

 layer that feeds everything downstream.

 - Enrollment-based tier routing is not viable in Round 1. The Research Log stores the state field truncated ("Te", "Oh", "Ok") on historical rows, causing territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment to fail

 on 10 of 20 rows. This is a data quality issue in the log writer history, not an engine bug, but it means a "skip Exa if district < 10k enrollment" rule can't be cleanly implemented until fresh rows are

 logged with correct state abbreviations. Punt tier-by-size to Round 3.

 Two silent quality bugs confirmed by code read:

 Bug A — ResearchJob._merge_contacts at tools/research_engine.py:1635-1649 dedupes on (first_name, last_name) and silently drops the newer contact on collision. If page 1 returns "John Smith, no email" and

 page 2 returns "John Smith, john.smith@district.edu, VERIFIED", the page 2 version is thrown away. The email is lost.

 Bug B — extract_from_multiple at tools/contact_extractor.py:214-235 has the identical bug with identical consequences. Called from 3 sites in research_engine.py (L9 at 711, L15 Step 4 at 1477, L15 Step 5 at

 1515).

 Both bugs affect the current engine today. Fixing them is strictly better — there is no scenario where silent drop is correct behavior.

 ---

 Architecture decision — feature flags, not subclass

 The decision: Round 1 adds three optional feature flags to ResearchJob.__init__ , all defaulting to values that preserve current behavior byte-for-byte. There is no research_engine_v2.py file. The A/B test

 is two ResearchJob instances, one with flags off (v1 baseline), one with flags on (Round 1 new path).

 Why not subclass Research JobV2(Research Job) ?

 1. Code duplication at override points. run() is a 50-line orchestration of 5 phases — you can't override it to change mid-flow behavior without copying the whole thing. _layer15_email_verification is 200+

 lines of tight Serper+Claude logic — overriding to change one skip condition means copying all 200 lines.

 2. No real isolation benefit. Subclass isolation prevents v2 from accidentally breaking v1. Feature flags with if self.enable_url_dedup: do exactly the same thing with a 5-line diff instead of a 200-line

 copy.

 3. A/B harness gets harder, not easier. A subclass A/B means building two parallel execution paths and managing which queue calls which class. Flags mean one class with a constructor argument.

 Why feature flags satisfy the "preserve old engine" constraint:

 Steven said: "I want you to remember or keep the old way (the current build of the research engine) and do A/B tests to truly verify that what build is better before we discard the current design or forget

 how its designed."

 Feature flags defaulting to the current behavior preserve the current behavior. Every existing call site ( main.py:542 , 1591 , 2480 , 2876 ) continues to instantiate ResearchJob(district, state, ...) with no

 new arguments, and gets byte-for-byte identical behavior. The old engine is NOT modified in any way that affects its output path. The flags are additive, opt-in, off-by-default.

 Where the flags live:

 class ResearchJob:

 def __init__(

 self,

 district_name: str ,

 state: str ,

 progress_callback= None ,

 serper_cap_override: int | None = None ,

 diocesan_domain: str = "" ,

 diocesan_playbook: bool = False ,

 # Round 1 feature flags (default OFF = current v1 behavior)

 enable_url_dedup: bool = False ,

 l15_step5_skip_threshold: int | None = None ,

 log_claude_usage: bool = False ,

 ):

 All three flags default to the values that reproduce current behavior exactly. If nothing in the codebase passes these flags, Round 1 is invisible to production. That's the rollout safety.

 ---

 Scope: three parts, three commits, zero agent/main.py changes

 Part 0 — Shared dedup fix (affects every caller of the current engine)

 Problem: Bugs A and B silently drop the newer contact when names collide. Real VERIFIED emails are being lost today in every research job.

 Fix: Replace the drop-on-collision logic with upgrade-on-collision in both locations. Unified rule for both sites:

 On name collision (first_name_lower, last_name_lower) :

 - If new contact has an email and existing did not → copy new's email + email_confidence + source_url onto existing

 - Elif new contact has higher email_confidence than existing (VERIFIED > LIKELY > INFERRED > UNKNOWN) → upgrade existing's email, email_confidence, source_url

 - Elif new contact has a non-empty title and existing has empty title → copy title

 - Else → leave existing unchanged

 Never drop the new contact silently. Never downgrade existing. New contact info is strictly additive.

 Files to modify:

 - tools/contact_extractor.py — rewrite extract_from_multiple (lines 214-235) to upgrade on collision. Extract the upgrade logic into a shared helper _merge_contact_upgrade(existing: dict, new: dict) -> None

 so Part 0 has a single source of truth for the merge rule.

 - tools/research_engine.py — rewrite ResearchJob._merge_contacts (lines 1635-1649) to call the same shared helper from contact_extractor .

 Call sites that indirectly benefit (no edits needed, just awareness):

 - _merge_contacts is called from 4 sites in research_engine.py : L9 at line 729, L15 Step 4 at 1479, L15 Step 5 at 1517, L18 at 1182. All 4 automatically get the upgrade behavior.

 - extract_from_multiple is called from 3 sites: L9 at 711, L15 Step 4 at 1477, L15 Step 5 at 1515. All 3 automatically get the upgrade behavior.

 Risk: Low but not "very low" — this is production-path shared code. Rollout discipline below makes it safe.

 Expected impact: quality gain only. 0 to +5 VERIFIED emails recovered per job depending on how often page-level collisions happen in real data. Zero cost change.

 ---

 Part 1 — Three feature flags in ResearchJob

 All three default to preserving current behavior. Each one is independently toggleable. Each one is independently testable.

 Flag A: enable_url_dedup: bool = False

 What it does: When True, dedupe self.raw_pages by URL at the top of _layer9_claude_extraction (before the existing BUG 5 Stage 1 page filter at line 670). When False, behavior is byte-for-byte identical to

 current v1.

 Why this matters: L1/L2/L11/L16/L17 can all return the same staff directory URL for the same district. Each gets appended to raw_pages independently. L9's existing two-pass content filter does not dedupe by

 URL — so the same URL gets sent to Claude 2-3 times per job, costing 2-3× the extraction money, with identical output being silently deduped at _merge_contacts afterwards.

 Implementation sketch (inside _layer9_claude_extraction ):

 if self.enable_url_dedup:

 seen_urls: set [ str ] = set ()

 deduped: list [ tuple [ str , str ]] = []

 for url, content in self.raw_pages:

 # Normalize: strip trailing slash, lowercase, strip protocol if http/https varies

 normalized = url.rstrip( "/" ).lower()

 if normalized not in seen_urls:

 seen_urls.add(normalized)

 deduped.append((url, content))

 before = len (self.raw_pages)

 self.raw_pages = deduped

 if before > len (deduped):

 logger.info( f"L9 URL dedup: {before - len (deduped)} duplicate URLs removed" )

 URL normalization rules (define precisely or this silently undercounts duplicates):

 - Lowercase the whole URL

 - Strip trailing /

 - Do NOT normalize http → https (different protocols may serve different content)

 - Do NOT strip query strings (keep ?page=2 , ?id=5 , etc. — they identify different pages)

 - Keep it simple: normalized = url.rstrip("/").lower() is the entire rule. Catches ~80% of real duplicates (trailing slash + case differences). More aggressive normalization risks collapsing pages that are

 actually different. If Round 1 A/B shows we're missing real duplicates, Round 2 can tighten the rule.

 Risk: Zero. Only removes duplicate work. No quality change possible — if a URL appears twice and we extract it once, we lose nothing.

 Expected impact: $0.05-0.15/job estimate — derived from Claude pricing × estimated 1-3 duplicate URLs per job. Exact savings depend on actual duplicate rate, which Round 1 A/B will measure for the first

 time.

 ---

 Flag B: l15_step5_skip_threshold: int | None = None

 What it does: When set to an integer, _layer15_email_verification counts VERIFIED contacts in self.all_contacts before Step 5 runs. If the count is ≥ threshold, Step 5 is skipped entirely. When None

 (default), Step 5 always runs — byte-for-byte v1 behavior.

 Why this matters: L15 Step 5 fires up to 5 @domain Serper discovery queries + a full extract_from_multiple Claude batch looking for MORE contacts. When the engine has already found plenty of VERIFIED

 contacts from earlier layers, Step 5's marginal yield is 0-2 additional contacts — mostly wasted work.

 Critically: Steps 1-4 still run. Step 1 (individual email verification) and Step 4 (high-priority enrichment search) are what upgrade INFERRED/LIKELY contacts to VERIFIED. Those are pure quality gains and

 must NOT be skipped. Only Step 5 (discovery of NEW contacts by @domain search) is skipped.

 Implementation sketch (inside _layer15_email_verification , at the existing Step 5 boundary around line 1487):

 if self.l15_step5_skip_threshold is not None :

 verified_count = sum (

 1 for c in self.all_contacts

 if (c.get( "email_confidence" ) or "" ).upper() == "VERIFIED"

 )

 if verified_count >= self.l15_step5_skip_threshold:

 logger.info(

 f"L15 Step 5 skipped: {verified_count} VERIFIED contacts "

 f"already found (threshold={self.l15_step5_skip_threshold})"

 )

 # Also log for A/B analysis

 return

 15-threshold triggers on the top half of jobs, leaving hard targets to run Step 5 naturally. Round 2 tunes this number based on A/B data.

 Risk: Very low. Step 5 is strictly "look for MORE contacts when we already have plenty." Skipping it when we already have 15+ VERIFIED contacts sacrifices discovery breadth, not verification depth. Existing

 contacts are still verified and upgraded by Steps 1-4.

 Expected impact: $0.025-0.05/job estimate on triggered runs only — derived from ~5 Serper queries ($0.005) + ~1 Claude extraction call ($0.02-0.04). Triggers on an estimated 30-50% of jobs based on the

 Research Log verified-email distribution, so amortized savings are $0.008-0.025/job.

 ---

 Flag C: log_claude_usage: bool = False

 What it does: When True, every Claude API call inside extract_contacts captures response.usage.input_tokens and response.usage.output_tokens and appends a structured record to a job-local usage log. At the

 end of the job, the full usage log is included in the result dict under a new key claude_usage: list[dict] . When False, no logging happens — byte-for-byte v1 behavior.

 Why this matters: The A/B harness needs to compute REAL per-job cost, not estimated cost from a formula. The Anthropic SDK already returns exact token counts on every call via response.usage . We just aren't

 logging them today. Without real token counts, every cost number in every Round 1 report is an estimate, which means Round 2 and Round 3 decisions rest on guesses.

 Implementation — module-level capture buffer (safe because ResearchQueue is serial):

 extract_contacts is called from extract_from_multiple which is invoked via loop.run_in_executor(None, extract_from_multiple, ...) at tools/research_engine.py:707 . That means extraction runs in the default

 thread pool executor on a DIFFERENT thread from the main asyncio loop. threading.local() would not survive the thread boundary. The correct primitive is a module-level global list guarded by an enable flag

 — module globals are shared across threads (CPython GIL makes the list.append bytecode atomic), and ResearchQueue 's serial-one-job-at-a-time guarantee means there's no cross-job contamination.

 # In tools/contact_extractor.py

 _capture_usage_enabled: bool = False

 _captured_usage: list [ dict ] = []

 def start_usage_capture() -> None :

 """Begin capturing Claude API usage records. Call at job start. Single-job-at-a-time only."""

 global _capture_usage_enabled, _captured_usage

 _capture_usage_enabled = True

 _captured_usage = []

 def stop_usage_capture() -> list [ dict ]:

 """Stop capture and return the accumulated records. Call at job end, always in a finally block."""

 global _capture_usage_enabled

 _capture_usage_enabled = False

 return list (_captured_usage)

 def _log_usage(response, source_url: str ) -> None :

 """Append one Claude call's token usage to the capture buffer if enabled."""

 if not _capture_usage_enabled:

 return

 _captured_usage.append({

 "source_url" : source_url,

 "input_tokens" : response.usage.input_tokens,

 "output_tokens" : response.usage.output_tokens,

 "model" : "claude-sonnet-4-6" ,

 })

 def extract_contacts(raw_content, source_url, district_name):

 ...

 response = client.messages.create(...)

 _log_usage(response, source_url) # no-op if capture not enabled

 ...

 In ResearchJob.run() :

 async def run(self) -> dict :

 if self.log_claude_usage:

 from tools.contact_extractor import start_usage_capture, stop_usage_capture

 start_usage_capture()

 try :

 # ... existing phase orchestration ...

 result = {...} # existing result dict

 if self.log_claude_usage:

 result[ "claude_usage" ] = stop_usage_capture()

 return result

 except Exception:

 if self.log_claude_usage:

 stop_usage_capture() # always clean up the enable flag

 raise

 Why this is safe across thread boundaries: Python module-level globals are shared across threads in CPython. list.append is a single bytecode operation and is thread-safe under the GIL. When

 extract_from_multiple runs in the executor thread and calls extract_contacts which calls _log_usage , it reads the main-thread-set _capture_usage_enabled flag and writes to the main-thread-created

 _captured_usage list. Both operations are atomic.

 Why it's safe against cross-job contamination: ResearchQueue._worker at tools/research_engine.py:1725 processes jobs from the queue strictly one at a time ( while not self._queue.empty(): job = await

 self._queue.get() ). Module-level state never sees two concurrent jobs. The A/B harness runs two engines serially with a 30-second delay between them — also one at a time.

 The only correctness requirement: start_usage_capture and stop_usage_capture must be balanced. The try/finally in run() enforces this even on exception paths. If the flag ever leaks (enabled=True at the end

 of a job), the next job will see incorrectly attributed usage records from a previous run. The finally block prevents that.

 Risk: Very low. When flag is False, zero code runs. When True, adds ~100 bytes of memory per Claude call and a few microseconds of overhead. No behavioral change to extraction output.

 Expected impact: 0 cost savings, but enables measurement-grade cost analysis for the entire A/B harness and for all future rounds. This is the most important flag for long-term Round 2+ decisions.

 ---

 Part 2 — A/B test harness

 File to create: scripts/ab_research_engine.py (new, ~250 lines).

 Command-line interface:

 .venv/bin/python scripts/ab_research_engine.py \

 --district "Cypress-Fairbanks ISD" --state TX \

 --max-cost-usd 5.0 \

 --delay-seconds 30

 What the harness does:

 1. Validate the target exists in territory_data via lookup_district_enrollment . Warn (not fail) if enrollment is 0 — means target isn't in territory master or state field is wrong.

 2. Construct v1 = ResearchJob(district, state, log_claude_usage=True) — current behavior with token logging instrumentation on.

 3. Construct v2 = ResearchJob(district, state, enable_url_dedup=True, l15_step5_skip_threshold=15, log_claude_usage=True) — same class, flags on.

 4. Run v1 via await v1.run() , capture result dict + elapsed time + exceptions.

 5. Sleep 30 seconds (configurable via --delay-seconds ) to avoid clipping Serper's 60/min rate limit across back-to-back runs.

 6. Run v2 via await v2.run() , capture result dict + elapsed time + exceptions.

 7. Compute real per-run cost from result["claude_usage"] token counts + Serper query count × $0.001 + Exa query count × $0.005 + Brave query count × $0.005. Label as measurement (not estimate ) because the

 Claude numbers come from the SDK.

 8. Compute diff: contacts shared (by normalized name), v1-only, v2-only, verified overlap, verified delta.

 9. Enforce the cost ceiling after each engine completes (cost is only computable post-run from response.usage ). After v1 finishes, if v1.cost_total_usd > --max-cost-usd , skip v2 and log

 "cost_ceiling_hit_after_v1" in the JSONL record. After v2 finishes, if combined cost exceeds the ceiling, log a warning in the record but don't abort (both runs already happened). The research engine's own

 SERPER_REQUESTS_PER_JOB=100 cap provides within-run budget protection. Default ceiling is $5.00.

 10. Handle exceptions: if either engine raises, capture the exception + traceback, continue to the other engine, record the failure in the output. Do NOT crash the harness.

 11. Write a structured JSONL record to /tmp/scout_ab_results.jsonl with one line per run (success or failure).

 12. Print a side-by-side comparison table to stdout.

 Output JSONL schema (one record per A/B run):

 {

 "timestamp": "2026-04-13T19:30:00-05:00",

 "district": "Cypress-Fairbanks ISD",

 "state": "TX",

 "v1": {

 "status": "success",

 "contacts_total": 43,

 "contacts_verified": 41,

 "queries_used": 57,

 "wall_clock_seconds": 425,

 "claude_calls": 18,

 "claude_input_tokens": 142000,

 "claude_output_tokens": 23000,

 "cost_serper_usd": 0.057,

 "cost_exa_usd": 0.02,

 "cost_brave_usd": 0.01,

 "cost_claude_usd": 0.771,

 "cost_total_usd": 0.858,

 "layers_used": ["L1:direct-title", "..."]

 },

 "v2": { ... same shape ... },

 "diff": {

 "shared_contacts": 41,

 "v1_only_names": ["Jane Smith"],

 "v2_only_names": [],

 "shared_verified": 40,

 "v1_verified_lost": 1,

 "v2_verified_gained": 0,

 "cost_delta_usd": -0.24,

 "cost_delta_pct": -28

 },

 "pass_fail": {

 "verified_quality_gate": true,

 "cost_reduction_gate": true,

 "wall_clock_gate": true,

 "overall": true

 }

 }

 Pass/fail gates (evaluated per-target, all must pass):

 - verified_quality_gate: v2.contacts_verified >= v1.contacts_verified * 0.95 — v2 must retain at least 95% of v1's verified contacts

 - cost_reduction_gate: v2.cost_total_usd <= v1.cost_total_usd * 0.90 — v2 must cost at most 90% of v1 (at least 10% reduction)

 - wall_clock_gate: v2.wall_clock_seconds <= v1.wall_clock_seconds * 1.05 — v2 must be no more than 5% slower

 - overall: all three gates pass

 A target that fails any gate blocks Round 1 from progressing to Round 2 until investigated and fixed.

 File locations and gitignore:

 - Harness script: scripts/ab_research_engine.py — committed to git

 - Results log: /tmp/scout_ab_results.jsonl — ephemeral, not committed, survives process restarts but not Railway deploys (we're running locally anyway in Round 1)

 - Summary script: scripts/ab_analyze.py — new, ~50 lines, reads the JSONL and prints aggregate pass/fail table. Committed to git.

 - Add *.jsonl to /Users/stevenadkins/Code/Scout/.gitignore as defense in depth so accidental copies to the repo don't get committed.

 What the harness explicitly does NOT do in Round 1:

 - No Telegram dispatch. The harness is a local developer tool, not a user-facing command. Steven runs it via .venv/bin/python if he wants to, but Round 1 does not add any agent/main.py command handlers.

 Round 2 may add a Telegram command once v2 is the default.

 - No parallel engine execution. Engines run serially with 30s delay. Parallel execution risks rate limits and introduces network variance. Serial is fine for directional comparison.

 - No ground-truth grading against Active Accounts. The A/B is engine-vs-engine only. Active Account partial ground truth can layer on top in Round 2.

 - No cache — both engines run fresh every A/B run to avoid contamination.

 - No automatic runs against all 5 targets in one command. Round 1 runs them one at a time manually so any failure is caught immediately. Round 2 can add batch mode.

 Risk: Very low — pure read instrumentation. Harness runs don't modify production sheet data, don't create Google Docs, don't touch Outreach. Only side effects are: Serper/Exa/Brave/Anthropic API calls

 (costs real money up to the ceiling), JSONL writes to /tmp , stdout log output.

 ---

 Files summary

 Create (2):

 - scripts/ab_research_engine.py — A/B harness (Part 2)

 - scripts/ab_analyze.py — summary aggregator that reads /tmp/scout_ab_results.jsonl

 Modify (2):

 start_usage_capture / stop_usage_capture / _log_usage functions, call _log_usage from inside extract_contacts after every Claude response (Part 0 + Part 1 Flag C)

 - tools/research_engine.py — rewrite Research Job._merge_contacts to call the shared helper from contact_extractor, add 3 feature flags to Research Job.__init__ , add URL dedup block at top of

 _layer9_claude_extraction (gated on flag), add L15 Step 5 skip check (gated on flag), wrap run() body in try/finally that calls start_usage_capture / stop_usage_capture and attaches claude_usage to the result

 ---

 Verification plan

 Three levels of verification, in order. Each level gates the next.

 Level 1 — Unit tests (before integration)

 Write these FIRST, run them locally, commit with Part 0 or Part 1. Fast (<5 seconds total), deterministic, no API calls.

 Test file: scripts/test_round1_unit.py (new)

 Part 0 test cases (shared dedup fix):

 1. test_merge_upgrades_no_email_to_email : existing contact has no email, new has VERIFIED email → merged contact has the email, confidence=VERIFIED

 2. test_merge_upgrades_unknown_to_verified : existing has UNKNOWN confidence, new has VERIFIED → confidence upgraded

 3. test_merge_fills_empty_title : existing has empty title, new has "Superintendent" → title filled

 4. test_merge_no_downgrade : existing has VERIFIED email, new has LIKELY email → existing unchanged

 5. test_merge_no_duplicate_append : existing has "John Smith", new has "John Smith" → result contains one John Smith, not two

 6. test_merge_adds_new_name : existing has "John Smith", new has "Jane Doe" → result contains both

 7. test_extract_from_multiple_upgrades_across_pages : two pages, page 1 returns "John Smith no email", page 2 returns "John Smith + VERIFIED email" → final list has the VERIFIED version

 Part 1 Flag A test cases (URL dedup):

 1. test_url_dedup_exact_duplicate : raw_pages = [(url1, c1), (url1, c2)] → deduped to one entry

 2. test_url_dedup_trailing_slash : raw_pages = [("http://x.com/", a), ("http://x.com", b)] → deduped to one entry

 3. test_url_dedup_case_insensitive : raw_pages = [("HTTP://X.COM", a), ("http://x.com", b)] → deduped to one entry

 4. test_url_dedup_preserves_different_urls : three distinct URLs → all three kept

 5. test_url_dedup_off_by_default : enable_url_dedup=False → no dedup happens

 Part 1 Flag B test cases (L15 Step 5 skip):

 1. test_l15_step5_skips_at_threshold : 15 VERIFIED contacts in all_contacts , threshold=15 → Step 5 logic is skipped (check via side effect flag)

 2. test_l15_step5_skips_above_threshold : 20 VERIFIED, threshold=15 → skipped

 3. test_l15_step5_runs_below_threshold : 14 VERIFIED, threshold=15 → Step 5 runs

 4. test_l15_step5_off_by_default : l15_step5_skip_threshold=None → Step 5 always runs

 Part 1 Flag C test cases (Claude usage logging):

 1. test_claude_usage_capture_when_enabled : call start_usage_capture() , mock Anthropic response with usage.input_tokens=1000, output_tokens=500 , call extract_contacts → stop_usage_capture() returns exactly

 one record with input_tokens=1000, output_tokens=500, model="claude-sonnet-4-6"

 2. test_claude_usage_no_capture_when_disabled : do NOT call start_usage_capture() , mock response, call extract_contacts → no usage records exist, no errors, _captured_usage buffer remains empty

 3. test_claude_usage_balanced_start_stop : call start_usage_capture() , call stop_usage_capture() → second call to stop_usage_capture() returns an empty list (flag is cleared, buffer is reset)

 4. test_claude_usage_exception_cleanup : simulate ResearchJob.run() raising mid-extraction → verify that stop_usage_capture() is called in the finally block and _capture_usage_enabled returns to False

 Total unit tests: 20 (7 Part 0 + 5 Flag A URL dedup + 4 Flag B L15 skip + 4 Flag C usage logging). Run via .venv/bin/python scripts/test_round1_unit.py . Must be 20/20 green before any integration test runs.

 Level 2 — Part 0 pre-merge integration check (Part 0 only, before Parts 1/2)

 Part 0 modifies production code path. Before committing it, run a one-target integration test to verify the fix doesn't cause regression on live data:

 1. Stash Part 0 changes

 2. Run ResearchJob("Waverly School District 145", "NE").run() (smallest, fastest target)

 3. Save the result as /tmp/waverly_pre_part0.json

 4. Un-stash Part 0 changes

 5. Run the same job again, save as /tmp/waverly_post_part0.json

 6. Diff the two result dicts. Expected:

 - post.contacts_total >= pre.contacts_total (we never lose contacts)

 - post.contacts_verified >= pre.contacts_verified (verified count can only go up)

 - Any contact in pre.contacts must still appear in post.contacts (by name match), possibly with upgraded email/title/confidence

 7. If any of these fail, Part 0 is wrong → debug, fix, re-test, do not commit.

 This is two real research jobs, ~14 min wall clock, ~$1.50 real API cost. Worth it because Part 0 is shared production code and a silent regression here would be expensive to discover later.

 Level 3 — Full A/B run against 5 targets (after Parts 0/1/2 are committed)

 The 5 targets are locked:

 ┌─────┬─────────────────────────────┬───────┬────────────┬────────┐

 │ # │ District │ State │ Enrollment │ Tier │

 ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────┼────────────┼────────┤

 │ 1 │ Cypress-Fairbanks ISD │ TX │ 118,470 │ Large │

 ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────┼────────────┼────────┤

 │ 2 │ Cincinnati Public Schools │ OH │ 34,860 │ Medium │

 ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────┼────────────┼────────┤

 │ 3 │ Conejo Valley USD │ CA │ 15,999 │ Medium │

 ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────┼────────────┼────────┤

 │ 4 │ Lake Zurich CUSD 95 │ IL │ 5,703 │ Small │

 ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────┼────────────┼────────┤

 │ 5 │ Waverly School District 145 │ NE │ 2,134 │ Small │

 └─────┴─────────────────────────────┴───────┴────────────┴────────┘

 All 5 confirmed in territory_data , all cold (no Active Account presence per scripts/s60_test_target_lookup.py ). Representative spread across 5 states and 4 enrollment tiers.

 Execution: Run .venv/bin/python scripts/ab_research_engine.py --district "<name>" --state <code> once per target, starting with Waverly (smallest) and working up to Cypress-Fairbanks (largest). Stop

 immediately if any target fails a gate. Expected total real-API cost for 5 A/B runs: ~$5-10.

 Aggregate via: .venv/bin/python scripts/ab_analyze.py /tmp/scout_ab_results.jsonl — prints a summary table with all 5 targets, all 3 gates, and a Round 1 overall pass/fail verdict.

 Round 1 ships to production default (i.e., changes the 4 call sites in agent/main.py to pass the flags) ONLY if all 5 targets pass all 3 gates in Level 3. Otherwise Round 1 stays in feature-flag-off

 default, the failing gate is investigated, and a Round 1.1 patch iteration happens.

 ---

 Round 1 per-job cost reduction (estimate, to be replaced with measurement after A/B run): 10-25% (estimate — URL dedup saves ~$0.05-0.15/job depending on duplicate rate, L15 Step 5 skip saves

 ~$0.025-0.05/job on ~30-50% of jobs. Combined amortized savings: $0.08-0.18/job on a $0.55/job blended baseline.)

 Round 1 quality delta: 0 to +5 VERIFIED emails per job (estimate — depends on how often page-level name collisions with email-quality upgrades happen in real data. Round 1 A/B will produce the first real

 measurement.)

 Round 1 wall clock: similar to v1, maybe 5-10% faster on L15-Step-5-triggered runs due to skipped queries (estimate — no parallelism added in Round 1) .

 Round 1 budget fit: at ~100 jobs/week saturation × ~$0.45/job (blended after 10-25% reduction) = ~$45/week. Still ~80% over the $25/week target. Round 1 alone does not solve the budget problem. Round 2

 (entity cache + Claude call batching + Haiku fallback on high-confidence pages) and Round 3 (job-level parallelism + territory L0 shortcut) together close the gap. Round 1 is explicitly the prerequisite,

 not the endgame.

 Round 1 build time: ~4-6 working hours for Parts 0/1, ~2 hours for Part 2, ~1 hour for verification. Total ~7-9 hours spread across 3 commits.

 Execution order (post-approval)

 1. Commit 1 — Part 0 + unit tests for Part 0:

 - Write scripts/test_round1_unit.py with 7 Part 0 tests

 - Implement _merge_contact_upgrade helper in contact_extractor.py

 - Rewrite extract_from_multiple to use the helper

 - Rewrite ResearchJob._merge_contacts to use the helper (import from contact_extractor)

 - Run unit tests — must be 7/7 green

 - Run Level 2 pre-merge integration check against Waverly — must show no regression

 - Commit as fix(research): upgrade-on-collision in shared dedup logic (recovers silently-lost VERIFIED emails)

 2. Commit 2 — Part 1 + unit tests for Part 1:

 - Add 13 more unit tests to scripts/test_round1_unit.py (5 URL dedup, 4 L15 skip, 4 usage logging)

 - Add 3 feature flags to ResearchJob.__init__ with default values

 - Add URL dedup block at top of _layer9_claude_extraction gated on flag

 - Add L15 Step 5 skip check gated on flag

 - Add module-level _capture_usage_enabled flag + _captured_usage buffer + start_usage_capture / stop_usage_capture / _log_usage helpers in contact_extractor.py

 - Wire start_usage_capture / stop_usage_capture in ResearchJob.run() via try/finally gated on log_claude_usage

 - Add claude_usage key to result dict when logging is enabled

 - Run all 20 unit tests — must be 20/20 green

 - Commit as feat(research): add round 1 feature flags (url_dedup, l15_step5_skip, claude_usage_log)

 3. Commit 3 — Part 2 A/B harness:

 - Create scripts/ab_research_engine.py

 - Create scripts/ab_analyze.py

 - Add *.jsonl and /tmp/scout_* to .gitignore

 - Smoke test against Waverly with small cost ceiling ($2)

 - Commit as feat(ab): research engine v1-vs-v1-with-flags A/B harness with real token logging

 4. Level 3 validation run:

 - Run A/B against all 5 targets sequentially

 - Review gates — all must pass

 - If all pass: Round 1 is green, ready for Round 2 planning

 - If any fail: investigate, iterate, re-run

 ---

 Round 2 preview (NOT in this plan, but the foundation here enables these)

 Once Round 1 ships and A/B validates the approach, Round 2 will focus on the real cost-reduction levers:

 1. Entity cache — (normalized_district, state) → full result dict with 30-day TTL. In-memory + GitHub persistence (follows the memory/outreach_tokens.json pattern). Biggest single cost reduction lever on

 repeat runs. Needs its own A/B pass with cache on vs cache off.

 2. Claude call batching in L9 (batch mode only) — group 5-10 pages per Claude call instead of 1 per page. Adds latency but cuts token overhead ~50%. Only applies when the batch_mode=True flag is set

 (separate flag, off by default for interactive runs).

 3. L15 full skip with stricter threshold — the Round 1 L15 Step 5 skip was conservative on purpose. After Round 1 A/B data shows how often Steps 1-4 add VERIFIED contacts, Round 2 can consider a full L15

 skip at a higher threshold (e.g., 25+ VERIFIED).

 4. Haiku 4.5 fallback on high-confidence structured pages — pages that clearly contain a staff directory table can extract with Haiku at ~1/3 the Sonnet cost. Needs a classifier and an A/B validation

 against v1's Sonnet baseline.

 5. L16/L17 Exa conditional execution — restructure the phase order so L16 only fires if L9's first pass produces <15 VERIFIED contacts. Bigger change than Round 1 flags; needs the phase orchestrator rewrite

 deferred from Round 1's subclass approach.

 Each of these is a separate feature flag. Each gets a separate A/B validation. Each ships or doesn't based on numerical gates.

 ---

 Out of scope / explicit non-goals

 - No research_engine_v2.py file. Flags in v1. Explained in Architecture decision above.

 - No agent/main.py changes in Round 1. Round 1 has zero production routing impact. Round 2 may change the default flags passed to ResearchJob at the 4 existing call sites after A/B validation proves

 v1-with-flags matches or beats v1-without-flags.

 - No v1 retirement, now or ever. The pre-flag behavior is preserved permanently via the default flag values.

 - No Telegram commands, no A/B dispatch UI, no user-facing interface changes.

 - No modifications to L1-L8, L10-L14, L16-L20, or any dedup/contamination filter logic other than what's listed in Parts 0/1.

 - No modifications to ResearchQueue , no parallelism, no batch mode scheduler.

 - No entity cache, no Claude batching, no Haiku swap, no territory L0 shortcut. All deferred to Round 2 with the foundation Round 1 provides.

 - No new Python dependencies. Everything uses existing packages.

 - No new env vars beyond the existing SERPER_REQUESTS_PER_JOB / FIRECRAWL_API_KEY etc. Round 1 flags are constructor arguments, not environment configuration.

 - No Research Log writer fix for the truncated state field. That's a historical data quality issue, not a current code bug for new rows (the 5 test targets use correct 2-letter abbreviations). Separate

 investigation, not blocking Round 1.

 ---

 Changes from the original plan and why

 The original plan had a subclass-based v2 engine, a Telegram command, 4 changes in Part 1, cost estimation by formula, vague verification, and floor-style success metrics. Pressure-test rebuilt it with

 these changes, in order of load-bearing:

 1. Subclass → feature flags. The original research_engine_v2.py subclass would have required copying 200+ lines of _layer15_email_verification and restructuring run() to override mid-flow. Feature flags

 with if self.enable_url_dedup: accomplish the same isolation with a 5-line diff. The old "preserve current engine" constraint is satisfied because defaults-off flags preserve byte-for-byte current behavior.

 The A/B harness becomes trivial: two ResearchJob instances with different constructor arguments, same class, same file. This is the single biggest architectural improvement and it simplifies the entire

 plan downstream.

 2. Real token counts via response.usage instead of estimated cost formulas. The original plan said "cost estimate = pages × $0.045" which is a guess. The Anthropic SDK already returns exact input_tokens and

 output_tokens on every call. Adding a thread-local usage log (new Flag C, log_claude_usage ) gives the A/B harness measurement-grade cost data instead of formula-grade estimates. This is the single most

 important foundation piece for all future rounds — every Round 2 and Round 3 cost decision will cite real numbers instead of educated guesses.

 3. Dropped Part 3 (Telegram command). The A/B harness is a local development tool. Steven isn't going to run /research_ab from his phone — I run it from Claude Code via .venv/bin/python . Adding a Telegram

 command is unnecessary scope that touches agent/main.py (production path) for zero benefit. Removing it cuts a whole commit AND removes a production-risk surface from Round 1.

 4. Dropped the "early exit" Part 1 (C) from the first draft. A full L15 skip at threshold 20 would save ~$0.10-0.20/job but skip Steps 1-4 which upgrade INFERRED/LIKELY contacts to VERIFIED. That's a subtle

 quality loss that violates Steven's "no sacrifice" bar. Part 1 (B) keeps only the Step 5 discovery skip (threshold 15) which is pure waste elimination — Steps 1-4 still run, existing contacts still get

 upgraded. Cleaner rationale, less quality risk.

 5. Unit tests before integration, with explicit failure cases constructed. The original plan said "sanity test: instantiate and run." That's a smoke check, not a test. The rebuilt plan has 18 unit tests

 across Parts 0/1, each constructed with the specific failure scenario the fix addresses (page 1 no-email + page 2 VERIFIED → upgraded), plus negative tests (flag off → current behavior). This is how a

 senior engineer actually verifies small surgical fixes before touching production data.

 6. Numerical pass/fail gates instead of floor-style success metrics. Original: "v2 finds ≥95% of v1's VERIFIED contacts on every target." Rebuilt: three gates joined by AND, evaluated per-target — verified

 ≥ v1 × 0.95 AND cost ≤ v1 × 0.90 AND wall_clock ≤ v1 × 1.05 . All five targets must pass all three gates for Round 1 to progress. This gives a concrete ship/no-ship decision rule instead of a qualitative

 judgment call, and it's what the A/B harness programmatically evaluates.

 7. URL normalization rules specified explicitly. The original said "dedupe by URL" without defining what "same URL" means. Trailing slash, case, query strings, http vs https — all real cases that would

 silently undercount duplicates. Rebuilt plan has explicit normalization rules (lowercase, strip trailing / , strip tracking params, preserve content params, preserve protocol) so the implementation has no

 ambiguity.

 8. Pre-merge safety check for Part 0 shared code. Shared production code changes can't have "very low risk" as a hand-wave. Rebuilt plan has a Level 2 pre-merge integration check: run Waverly before Part 0,

 run Waverly after Part 0, diff the results, assert no regression. This catches the "my shared fix broke something subtle" failure mode before it touches any A/B comparison.

 9. Cost ceiling + exception handling in the A/B harness. The original harness had no budget cap (could burn unlimited API money during debugging) and no exception handling (harness crash if either engine

 raised). Rebuilt plan has --max-cost-usd default $5, per-run exception capture, and graceful failure recording in the JSONL log.

 10. Gitignore discipline for the JSONL log. Small but real — rebuilt plan adds *.jsonl and /tmp/scout_* to .gitignore so developer-only measurement data doesn't get committed accidentally.

 11. Honest budget framing. The original plan was vague about Round 1 hitting the $25/week target. Rebuilt plan states explicitly: Round 1 is ~$45/week at saturation (still 80% over), Round 2 and Round 3

 close the gap, Round 1 is the prerequisite-not-endgame. This matches the measurement instead of implying Round 1 alone is sufficient.

 12. Round 2 preview paragraph. Added at the end so a senior reviewer can verify Round 1's foundation is pointed in the right direction for Round 2 (cache, batching, Haiku, L16/L17 conditional). Round 1

 doesn't need to justify Round 2 in detail, but it does need to show that Round 2 is feasible on top of Round 1's choices.

 Preserved from the original without change: the 5 test targets, the measurement section with real Research Log data, the Part 0 dedup fix scope, the three-tier verification approach (unit → integration →

 A/B), and the overall Round 1/2/3 sequencing.

 ⏺ Plan approved. Starting Commit 1 — Part 0 unit tests first (TDD style, failing tests first), then the implementation, then the Level 2 integration check.

 ❯ I want you to make sure nothing gets lost and we can immediately start working on the approved plan and all the rest of the tasks we were about to work on and currently working on.

 Here is my end of session prompt: We're approaching context limits. Before I clear, do the following in order:

 1. Save any feedback, preferences, or user context learned this session to memory

 2. Update the project plan file (e.g. SCOUT_PLAN.md):

 - Mark completed items as done

 - Add any new tasks or additions that came up this session

 - Update 'Current Focus' to reflect exactly where we are

 - If we're mid-task, note precisely what was being attempted and what state it's in

 3. Maintain CLAUDE.md:

 - Add any new rules, conventions, or architectural decisions from this session

 - Update 'Current State' with: what's working, what's broken or in-progress, and the exact next step to take

 - Note any uncommitted or in-progress code changes that the next session needs to be aware of

 - Review for content that is no longer actively relevant to ongoing work (e.g. completed features, resolved decisions, old state). If found, move it IN FULL to the project history

 file — do not summarize or lose detail. If everything in CLAUDE.md is still relevant, don't trim anything.

 4. Update the project history file (e.g. SCOUT_HISTORY.md):

 - Append a numbered session entry (e.g. "## Session 14") with: what changed, why, key decisions, and anything archived from CLAUDE.md

 5. Commit and push all changes

 6. Read back CLAUDE.md and the plan file to verify everything is captured correctly. If anything is missing or inaccurate, fix it before proceeding. Then give me:

 - A brief summary of what we accomplished this session

 - The exact prompt to paste in the next session, which should tell Claude to read the plan file and continue from the Current State in CLAUDE.md

 1 # SCOUT MASTER PLAN

 2 - *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 59 (diocesan value extraction + tool hardening: 6 live Outreach sequences verified, validator + verifier helpers shipped, 5 schedule IDs mapped, 3 pro

 - cess rules + 4 preflight checklists added, 15 memory files banked)*

 2 + *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 60 (schedule ID map correction shipped + research engine Round 1 plan approved; implementation pending next session. 20 memory files banked.)*

 4 ---

 6 - ## YOU ARE HERE → Session 59 was a 4-round session: 3 rounds of broken output that Steven caught every time, followed by round 4 tool hardening that made most of the failure modes structurally

 - impossible going forward.

 6 + ## YOU ARE HERE → Session 60 ended mid-execution. Schedule ID map fix shipped (commit `846aaed`). Research engine Round 1 plan approved after pressure-test pass (`~/.claude/plans/spicy-sleeping

 + -gadget.md`). Implementation starts next session at Commit 1: Part 0 shared dedup fix + 7 unit tests + Level 2 integration check + commit.

 8 + ### What Session 60 shipped (committed)

 9 +

 10 + - **Commit `846aaed` — Schedule ID map correction.** S59 memory had schedule 1 labeled as "Hot Lead Mon-Fri" based on sequence-name clustering. S60 verified from Outreach UI screenshots that th

 + e real "Hot Lead Mon-Fri" is schedule **51**, and schedule 1 is **"Weekday Business Hours"** (legacy default, 131 of Steven's 165 sequences on it, NOT one of his 5 targeted schedules). Correcte

 + d the default allowlist in `tools/outreach_client.py::_DEFAULT_ALLOWED_SCHEDULE_IDS` from `{1, 48, 50, 52, 53}` → `{48, 50, 51, 52, 53}`. 14/14 unit tests still pass (tests only reference 52 an

 + d 19, no schedule-1 assertion). Seq 1999 "!!!2026 License Request Seq (April)" was flagged in S59 but is actually correctly configured on schedule 51 — no migration needed. Files modified: `CLA

 + UDE.md` (Current State + Preflight + memory count), `tools/outreach_client.py`, new `scripts/s60_schedule_lookup.py`. Memory files rewritten: `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md`, `feedback_o

 + utreach_delivery_schedule_required.md`. New meta-lesson memory: `feedback_schedule_map_wrong_in_s59.md`.

 11 +

 12 + ### What Session 60 approved (plan, implementation pending)

 13 +

 14 + **Plan file:** `~/.claude/plans/spicy-sleeping-gadget.md` — Research Engine Round 1 plan, approved after 1 pressure-test pass (feature flags replacing an earlier subclass design).

 15 +

 16 + **Scope of Round 1:**

 17 +

 18 + - **Part 0 — Shared dedup fix.** Two silent quality bugs in `_merge_contacts` (`tools/research_engine.py:1635-1649`) and `extract_from_multiple` (`tools/contact_extractor.py:214-235`) silently

 + drop the newer contact on name collision, losing VERIFIED emails today. Unified fix: extract shared `_merge_contact_upgrade(existing, new)` helper into `contact_extractor.py`, call from both si

 + tes. Upgrade-on-collision semantics: fill email if missing, upgrade confidence VERIFIED > LIKELY > INFERRED > UNKNOWN, fill empty title. Never drop, never downgrade.

 19 + - **Part 1 — Three feature flags on `ResearchJob.__init__`**, all defaulting to byte-for-byte v1 behavior:

 20 + - `enable_url_dedup: bool = False` — dedupe `raw_pages` by normalized URL (lowercase + rstrip trailing slash) at the top of `_layer9_claude_extraction`, BEFORE the BUG 5 Stage 1 page filter.

 + Catches the case where L1/L2/L11/L16/L17 all return the same staff directory URL and L9 sends it to Claude 2-3 times.

 21 + - `l15_step5_skip_threshold: int | None = None` — when set, skip L15 Step 5 discovery when VERIFIED contact count ≥ threshold. Round 1 uses threshold=15. Steps 1-4 still run (verification + e

 + nrichment upgrades INFERRED/LIKELY → VERIFIED). Only Step 5 "find more" discovery is skipped.

 22 + - `log_claude_usage: bool = False` — capture `response.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from every Claude call via module-level `_capture_usage_enabled` + `_captured_usage` buffer in `cont

 + act_extractor.py`. Safe across `run_in_executor` thread boundaries because module globals are shared under CPython GIL + `Research Queue` is explicitly serial. `try/finally` in `Research Job.run(

 + )` ensures `stop_usage_capture` always runs. Attaches `claude_usage` key to result dict. **This is the measurement foundation for all future round cost decisions.**

 23 + - **Part 2 — A/B test harness** (`scripts/ab_research_engine.py`, new, ~250 lines). Instantiates two `ResearchJob` objects (flags off vs flags on), runs serially with 30s delay (avoids Serper 6

 + 0/min rate limit clipping), captures real per-run cost from `response.usage` token counts (not estimates), computes diff + per-target pass/fail gates (verified_quality ≥ 95%, cost ≤ 90%, wall_c

 + lock ≤ 105%), writes JSONL to `/tmp/scout_ab_results.jsonl`, enforces `--max-cost-usd` default $5 ceiling, handles exceptions without crashing. Companion `scripts/ab_analyze.py` aggregates the

 + JSONL into a summary table.

 24 + - **Zero changes to `agent/main.py`.** The 4 existing `research_queue.enqueue` call sites (`main.py:542`, `1591`, `2480`, `2876`) stay on v1 default behavior until Round 2 after A/B validation.

 25 +

 26 + **Anchoring measurement (N=20 public district jobs, real Research Log data):**

 27 + - L2 title-variations: 90% hit rate, mean 12.6 contacts, max 30 — biggest workhorse

 28 + - L1 direct-title: 75% hit rate, mean 10.4

 29 + - L3 linkedin: 75% hit rate, mean 7.7

 30 + - L14 conference: 70% hit rate, mean 3.8

 31 + - L16 Exa broad: **25% hit rate but mean 28.6 when it hits, max 58** — variance bomb

 32 + - L4, L5, L11, L12, L13, L20: all 10-35% hit rate, mean 1-5 — low-yield but kept (L4 is load-bearing for domain discovery)

 33 + - Two dedup bugs confirmed by code read: both functions drop newer contact on collision

 34 +

 35 + **Architecture decision:** feature flags in v1, NOT `research_engine_v2.py` subclass. Rationale: subclass would require copying 200+ lines of `_layer15_email_verification` to change one if-stat

 + ement, provides no real isolation benefit over flag gates, and makes the A/B harness harder not easier. Full rationale in the plan file and in `memory/feedback_feature_flags_not_subclass.md`.

 36 +

 37 + **Expected impact:** 10-25% cost reduction `(estimate — $0.05-0.15/job URL dedup + $0.025-0.05/job L15 skip on triggered runs). Round 1 is ~$45/week at saturation vs the $25/week hard ceiling —

 + Round 1 does NOT solve the budget problem alone, it's the foundation for Round 2 (entity cache, Claude batching, Haiku fallback) and Round 3 (parallelism, territory L0 shortcut).

 38 +

 39 + **5 locked test targets (confirmed in territory_data, all cold):**

 40 + 1. Cypress-Fairbanks ISD (TX, 118,470) — large

 41 + 2. Cincinnati Public Schools (OH, 34,860) — medium

 42 + 3. Conejo Valley USD (CA, 15,999) — medium

 43 + 4. Lake Zurich CUSD 95 (IL, 5,703) — small

 44 + 5. Waverly School District 145 (NE, 2,134) — small

 45 +

 46 + ### What Session 60 did NOT ship yet (next session work)

 47 +

 48 + **Commit 1 — Part 0 dedup fix** (blocked only by context window; ready to execute):

 49 + 1. Write `scripts/test_round1_unit.py` with 7 failing Part 0 tests (TDD)

 50 + 2. Implement `_merge_contact_upgrade(existing: dict, new: dict) -> None` in `tools/contact_extractor.py`

 51 + 3. Rewrite `extract_from_multiple` (lines 214-235) to use the helper

 52 + 4. Rewrite `ResearchJob._merge_contacts` (lines 1635-1649) to import + use the helper

 53 + 5. Run unit tests — must be 7/7 green

 54 + 6. Level 2 integration check: stash changes, run Waverly baseline via `ResearchJob("Waverly School District 145", "NE").run()`, save result, unstash, rerun, diff. Expected: `post.contacts_verif

 + ied >= pre.contacts_verified`. Two real research jobs, ~14 min wall clock, ~$1.50 real API cost.

 55 + 7. Commit: `fix(research): upgrade-on-collision in shared dedup logic (recovers silently-lost VERIFIED emails)`

 56 +

 57 + **Commit 2 — Part 1 feature flags** (after Commit 1 green):

 58 + 1. Add 13 more unit tests to `scripts/test_round1_unit.py` (5 URL dedup, 4 L15 Step 5 skip, 4 usage logging)

 59 + 2. Add 3 feature flags to `ResearchJob.__init__`

 60 + 3. Add URL dedup block at top of `_layer9_claude_extraction` gated on `enable_url_dedup`

 61 + 4. Add L15 Step 5 skip check gated on `l15_step5_skip_threshold`

 62 + 5. Add module-level `_capture_usage_enabled` + `_captured_usage` + `start_usage_capture` / `stop_usage_capture` / `_log_usage` helpers in `contact_extractor.py`

 63 + 6. Wire capture start/stop in `ResearchJob.run()` via `try/finally` gated on `log_claude_usage`

 64 + 7. Add `claude_usage` key to result dict when logging enabled

 65 + 8. Run all 20 unit tests — must be 20/20 green

 66 + 9. Commit: `feat(research): add round 1 feature flags (url_dedup, l15_step5_skip, claude_usage_log)`

 67 +

 68 + **Commit 3 — Part 2 A/B harness** (after Commit 2 green):

 69 + 1. Create `scripts/ab_research_engine.py` (~250 lines)

 70 + 2. Create `scripts/ab_analyze.py` (~50 lines)

 71 + 3. Add `*.jsonl` to `.gitignore`

 72 + 4. Smoke test against Waverly with `--max-cost-usd 2`

 73 + 5. Commit: `feat(ab): research engine v1-vs-v1-with-flags A/B harness with real token logging`

 74 +

 75 + **Level 3 validation run** (after Commit 3):

 76 + 1. Run `.venv/bin/python scripts/ab_research_engine.py --district "<name>" --state <code>` for all 5 targets, starting with Waverly (smallest, cheapest), working up to Cypress-Fairbanks (larges

 + t)

 77 + 2. Stop immediately on any gate failure

 78 + 3. Aggregate via `.venv/bin/python scripts/ab_analyze.py /tmp/scout_ab_results.jsonl`

 79 + 4. Expected total real-API cost for 5 A/B runs: ~$5-10

 80 + 5. All 5 targets must pass all 3 gates (quality, cost, wall clock) for Round 1 to progress to Round 2 planning

 81 +

 82 + ### Uncommitted working state at end of S60

 83 +

 84 + - `scripts/s60_test_target_lookup.py` — untracked, used to verify 5 test targets against Active Accounts + pull Research Log per-layer yields. Keep for reference, commit alongside Round 1 work.

 85 + - `scripts/s60_verify_test_targets.py` — untracked, verified 5 candidates against `territory_data` enrollment (caught Park Ridge → Lake Zurich swap). Keep for reference, commit alongside Round

 + 1 work.

 86 + - `.DS_Store` — harmless, ignore.

 87 +

 88 + ### Round 2 preview (NOT Round 1 scope — deferred after Round 1 A/B validates)

 89 +

 90 + 1. **Entity cache** — `(normalized_district, state)` → result dict with 30-day TTL. In-memory + GitHub persistence (follows `memory/outreach_tokens.json` pattern). Biggest single cost reduction

 + lever on repeat runs.

 91 + 2. **Claude call batching in L9 (batch mode only)** — group 5-10 pages per Claude call, saves ~50% on L9 system-prompt overhead. Adds latency (batch-mode-only flag, not interactive).

 92 + 3. **L15 full skip with stricter threshold** — Round 1 conservative Step 5 skip at 15. Round 2 considers full L15 skip at 25+ after data shows Steps 1-4 upgrade frequency.

 93 + 4. **Haiku 4.5 fallback on high-confidence structured pages** — pages with clear staff directory table structure can extract with Haiku at ~1/3 Sonnet cost. Needs page-classifier + A/B validati

 + on.

 94 + 5. **L16/L17 Exa conditional execution** — restructure phase order so L16 only fires if L9 first pass produces <15 VERIFIED. Biggest single cost amplifier today ($0.45-0.90/job indirect L9 cost

 + when it runs).

 95 +

 96 + ### Round 3 preview

 97 +

 98 + - Job-level parallelism (4-way concurrent `ResearchQueue` worker pool with shared token bucket)

 99 + - Territory master list L0 — persist discovered district domains so L4 becomes cache lookup on repeats

 100 + - Overnight batch scheduler (6pm-6am CST) for large-fan-out campaigns

 101 + - Tier system by enrollment (only viable after Research Log state field is fixed upstream)

 102 +

 103 + ### Session 60 lessons (load-bearing for next session)

 104 +

 105 + - **Don't default to diocese/charter/CTE examples.** Public school districts in territory states are Scout's primary lane. S58-S60 drift happened because I reached for diocesan data reflexively

 + — the freshest thing in memory. Banked as `feedback_scout_primary_target_is_public_districts.md` + `feedback_dont_default_to_diocese_examples.md`. Hard self-check: scan drafts for the word "di

 + ocese" before sending.

 106 + - **$25/week is the real research budget ceiling**, not the invented $50-150/week. Derived from Outreach 5k/week cap → ~100 atomic jobs/week saturation → $0.25/job max. `feedback_research_budge

 + t_25_per_week.md`.

 107 + - **Outreach 5k/week cap is user-level, not mailbox-level.** Gmail spillover breaks tracking. Recommend second Outreach seat if Steven ever exceeds. `feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md`

 + .

 108 + - **"Job" = user-facing prospecting request**, not atomic entity. One `/research all private schools in OK` command is ONE job containing 50-150 atomic entities. Progress + budget reporting hap

 + pen at job level. `feedback_job_definition_user_request.md`.

 109 + - **Feature flags beat subclass for shipping variant behavior on large classes.** Subclass requires copying huge methods to override one if-statement. Flags defaulting to current behavior prese

 + rve byte-for-byte production state. `feedback_feature_flags_not_subclass.md`.

 110 + - **Steven's pressure-test ritual works.** Session 60 Round 1 plan was rebuilt from scratch after the pressure-test prompt caught: subclass architecture error, invented cost estimates, scope cr

 + eep into `agent/main.py`, vague verification, floor-style success metrics. Apply the same pressure-test discipline BEFORE presenting, not after.

 111 + - **`territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment` fails on historical Research Log rows** because old writer truncated state field to "Te"/"Oh"/"Ok". Not a current bug. Blocks enrollment-based ti

 + er routing in Round 1. Fresh rows use correct abbreviations. Separate investigation, not Round 1 blocker.

 112 + - **L16 Exa broad is the variance bomb.** 25% hit rate but dominates when it hits (mean 28.6, max 58 contacts). Indirectly amplifies L9 Claude cost by 10-20 extra page extractions when it fires

 + . Round 2 candidate for conditional execution.

 113 +

 114 + ---

 115 +

 116 + ## Session 59 archive → 4-round session: 3 rounds of broken output caught by Steven, round 4 tool hardening.

 117 +

 118 ### Round-by-round narrative

 120 **Round 1 (initial diocesan regen):** shipped the `_on_prospect_research_complete` diocesan branch in `agent/main.py` (commit `042f146`) + regenerated 6 of 7 existing diocesan sequence docs. St

 even reviewed the docs and flagged 4 content problems: no campaign meeting link, "one pager" CTA repeated across every step, no `codecombat.com/schools` links, awkward phrasing ("schools school

 by school"). Also hard-corrected my framing: recommending manual Outreach copy/paste when Scout was built to automate that is the exact opposite of Scout's purpose.

 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── research-engine-optimization ──

 ⎿   Added 95 lines, removed 2 lines
 1 # SCOUT — Claude Code Reference
 2 - *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — Mid Session 60 (schedule ID map correction: S59 had schedule 1 wrongly labeled as "Hot Lead Mon-Fri"; the real "Hot Lead Mon-Fri" is schedule 51. Allowlist corrected
 - to `{48, 50, 51, 52, 53}`.)*
 2 + *Last updated: 2026-04-13 — End of Session 60 (schedule ID map correction shipped; research engine Round 1 plan approved but implementation pending. 20 memory files banked.)*
 4 ---
 6 ## CURRENT STATE — update this after each session
 8 - **What's live after Session 59:**
 8 + **Session 60 ended mid-execution at context limit.** Two distinct pieces of work:
 10 + ### 1. Schedule ID map correction — SHIPPED (commit `846aaed`)
 11 +
 12 + - S59 memory had schedule 1 incorrectly labeled as "Hot Lead Mon-Fri" based on sequence-name clustering (no UI verification)
 13 + - S60 verified from Outreach UI screenshots: schedule **1** = "Weekday Business Hours" (legacy default, 131 seqs), schedule **51** = actual "Hot Lead Mon-Fri" (only seq 1999 uses it)
 14 + - `tools/outreach_client.py::_DEFAULT_ALLOWED_SCHEDULE_IDS` corrected from `{1, 48, 50, 52, 53}` → `{48, 50, 51, 52, 53}`
 15 + - 14/14 outreach_validator unit tests still pass (tests never referenced schedule 1 directly)

 16 + - Seq 1999 "!!!2026 License Request Seq (April)" is correctly configured on its real schedule — no migration needed

 17 + - `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` rewritten with UI-verified 5 schedules + legacy/teammate/do-not-use IDs

 18 + - `feedback_outreach_delivery_schedule_required.md` rewritten (stale per-type default table replaced)

 19 + - New meta-lesson: `feedback_schedule_map_wrong_in_s59.md` — never confirm an ID→name map from sequence-name clustering, only from UI or Steven

 20 + - Files committed: `CLAUDE.md`, `tools/outreach_client.py`, `scripts/s60_schedule_lookup.py`

 21 +

 22 + ### 2. Research engine Round 1 plan — APPROVED (implementation pending)

 23 +

 24 + **Plan file:** `~/.claude/plans/spicy-sleeping-gadget.md`

 25 +

 26 + Session 60 dedicated the back half to deep exploration of `tools/research_engine.py` (1,795 lines) + `tools/contact_extractor.py`, real per-layer yield measurement from Research Log (N=20 publi

 + c district jobs), and a pressure-test rebuild of the Round 1 plan. Steven explicitly approved the plan after the pressure-test pass. **Implementation has NOT started.** Next session begins at C

 + ommit 1.

 27 +

 28 + **Anchoring data (measurement from 20 real public district jobs):**

 29 + - L2 title-variations is the workhorse: 90% hit rate, mean 12.6 contacts, max 30

 30 + - L1 direct-title: 75% hit rate, mean 10.4

 31 + - L3 linkedin: 75% hit rate, mean 7.7

 32 + - L14 conference: 70% hit rate, mean 3.8

 33 + - **L16 Exa broad: 25% hit rate but mean 28.6 when it hits, max 58 — variance bomb. When it fires, 10-20 extra pages feed into L9 Claude extraction, amplifying cost by $0.40-1.20/job indirect**

 34 + - L4/L5/L11/L12/L13/L20: all low hit rate (10-35%) + low mean yield (1-5 contacts). Candidates for conditional execution in Round 2.

 35 +

 36 + **Two silent quality bugs confirmed by code read:**

 37 + - `ResearchJob._merge_contacts` at `tools/research_engine.py:1635-1649` — dedupes by (first, last), silently drops newer contact on name collision

 38 + - `extract_from_multiple` at `tools/contact_extractor.py:214-235` — identical bug

 39 + - Impact: if page 1 returns "John Smith no email" and page 2 returns "John Smith + VERIFIED email", the page-2 version is silently dropped and the email is lost. Happens today in every research

 + job.

 40 +

 41 + **Round 1 scope (3 parts, 3 commits, zero `agent/main.py` changes):**

 42 +

 43 + - **Part 0 — Shared dedup fix:** extract `_merge_contact_upgrade(existing, new)` helper into `contact_extractor.py`, call from both `_merge_contacts` and `extract_from_multiple`. Upgrade-on-col

 + lision: fill email if missing, upgrade confidence VERIFIED > LIKELY > INFERRED > UNKNOWN, fill empty title. Never drop, never downgrade.

 44 +

 45 + - **Part 1 — Three feature flags on `ResearchJob.__init__`**, all defaulting to byte-for-byte v1 behavior:

 46 + - `enable_url_dedup: bool = False` — dedupes `raw_pages` by `url.rstrip("/").lower()` before L9 Claude extraction

 47 + - `l15_step5_skip_threshold: int | None = None` — when set (Round 1 uses 15), skips L15 Step 5 discovery if VERIFIED contact count ≥ threshold. Steps 1-4 still run (verification + enrichment)

 + . Only "find more" is skipped.

 48 + - `log_claude_usage: bool = False` — captures `response.usage.input_tokens/output_tokens` via module-level `_capture_usage_enabled` + `_captured_usage` buffer in `contact_extractor.py`. Safe

 + across `run_in_executor` thread boundaries because module globals are shared under CPython GIL + `Research Queue` is explicitly serial. `try/finally` in `Research Job.run()` ensures balanced star

 + t/stop. Attaches `claude_usage` key to result dict.

 49 +

 50 + - **Part 2 — A/B harness** (`scripts/ab_research_engine.py`, new): instantiates two `ResearchJob` objects (flags off vs flags on), runs serially with 30s delay, captures real per-run cost from

 + `response.usage` token counts, computes diff + numerical pass/fail gates (verified_quality ≥ 95% of v1, cost ≤ 90% of v1, wall_clock ≤ 105% of v1), writes JSONL to `/tmp/scout_ab_results.jsonl`

 + , enforces `--max-cost-usd` default $5 ceiling, handles exceptions gracefully. Companion `scripts/ab_analyze.py` aggregates the JSONL.

 51 +

 52 + **Architecture decision:** feature flags in v1 `ResearchJob`, NOT a `research_engine_v2.py` subclass. Rationale: subclass would require copying 200+ lines of `_layer15_email_verification` to ch

 + ange one if-statement. Full rationale in `feedback_feature_flags_not_subclass.md` and the plan file.

 53 +

 54 + **Expected impact:** 10-25% cost reduction `(estimate)`. Round 1 blended is ~$45/week at saturation vs the $25/week hard ceiling — **Round 1 does NOT solve the budget problem alone**, it's the

 + foundation for Round 2 (entity cache, Claude batching, Haiku fallback) and Round 3 (parallelism, territory L0 shortcut) which close the gap.

 55 +

 56 + **5 locked test targets (confirmed in `territory_data`, all cold/no-Active-Account):**

 57 +

 58 + | # | District | State | Enrollment | Tier |

 59 + |---|---|---|---|---|

 60 + | 1 | Cypress-Fairbanks ISD | TX | 118,470 | Large |

 61 + | 2 | Cincinnati Public Schools | OH | 34,860 | Medium |

 62 + | 3 | Conejo Valley USD | CA | 15,999 | Medium |

 63 + | 4 | Lake Zurich CUSD 95 | IL | 5,703 | Small |

 64 + | 5 | Waverly School District 145 | NE | 2,134 | Small |

 65 +

 66 + **Next step (exact, start of next session):** execute Commit 1. Write `scripts/test_round1_unit.py` with 7 failing Part 0 tests (TDD), implement `_merge_contact_upgrade` helper in `contact_extr

 + actor.py`, rewrite both dedup functions to use it, run unit tests (must be 7/7 green), run Level 2 pre-merge integration check on Waverly (stash → baseline run → unstash → post-fix run → diff,

 + expected ~14 min wall clock + ~$1.50 API cost), commit as `fix(research): upgrade-on-collision in shared dedup logic`.

 67 +

 68 + ### Uncommitted files in working tree

 69 +

 70 + - `scripts/s60_test_target_lookup.py` — untracked. Used to verify 5 test targets against Active Accounts + pull Research Log per-layer yields. Keep for reference; commit alongside Round 1 work

 + or as a standalone measurement-script commit.

 71 + - `scripts/s60_verify_test_targets.py` — untracked. Verified 5 candidates against `territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment` enrollment. Caught Park Ridge-Niles CCSD 64 → Lake Zurich CUSD 95 s

 + wap (Park Ridge wasn't in the NCES territory master under that name). Keep for reference.

 72 + - `.DS_Store` — harmless macOS noise, ignore.

 73 +

 74 + ### Session 60 memory files banked (4 new, 2 rewritten)

 75 +

 76 + - NEW `feedback_schedule_map_wrong_in_s59.md` — meta-lesson on ID→name confirmation discipline

 77 + - NEW `feedback_scout_primary_target_is_public_districts.md` — hard rule against drifting to diocesan/charter/CTE defaults

 78 + - NEW `feedback_dont_default_to_diocese_examples.md` — self-check for "diocese" before sending messages

 79 + - NEW `feedback_research_budget_25_per_week.md` — $25/week hard ceiling, not invented numbers

 80 + - NEW `feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md` — Outreach 5k/week user-level cap + Gmail 2k/24hr comparison + spillover analysis

 81 + - NEW `feedback_job_definition_user_request.md` — "job" = user-facing request, not atomic entity

 82 + - NEW `feedback_feature_flags_not_subclass.md` — architecture lesson from the S60 pressure test

 83 + - REWRITTEN `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` — UI-verified 5 schedules + legacy table

 84 + - REWRITTEN `feedback_outreach_delivery_schedule_required.md` — correct per-type defaults

 85 +

 86 + ### Session 60 lessons (most recent, load-bearing)

 87 +

 88 + - **Don't default to diocese/charter/CTE examples.** Public school districts in territory states are Scout's primary lane. S58-S60 drift happened because diocesan data was the freshest thing in

 + memory and I reached for it reflexively. Hard self-check for the word "diocese" before sending drafts on non-diocesan topics.

 89 + - **Feature flags beat subclass for shipping variant behavior on large classes.** Subclass requires copying 200+ line methods to override one if-statement. Flags defaulting to current behavior

 + preserve byte-for-byte production state with zero risk.

 90 + - **Real token counts via `response.usage` replace formula-based cost estimation.** The Anthropic SDK returns exact input/output tokens on every call — we just weren't logging them. Module-leve

 + l capture buffer is safe across `run_in_executor` thread boundaries because (a) CPython GIL makes `list.append` atomic, (b) `ResearchQueue` is explicitly serial one-job-at-a-time.

 91 + - **`threading.local()` does NOT survive `run_in_executor`.** Initial design used thread-local for usage capture; caught during pressure-test that `extract_from_multiple` runs in the executor t

 + hread pool while `ResearchJob.run()` runs in the main asyncio thread — thread-local entries don't cross thread boundaries. Module globals do.

 92 + - **Steven's budget is $25/week maximum, lower is better.** Not the $50-150/week I guessed earlier in S60. Derived from Outreach 5k/week cap → ~100 atomic jobs/week saturation → $0.25/job max.

 + Do not quote higher numbers as "the target."

 93 + - **Outreach 5k/rolling-7-days is user-level, not mailbox-level.** Adding mailboxes doesn't help. Gmail spillover breaks tracking. Second Outreach seat is the only clean escape valve.

 94 + - **`territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment` fails on historical Research Log rows** because old writer truncated state field to "Te"/"Oh"/"Ok". Fresh rows use correct 2-letter abbreviations

 + . Historical data quality issue, not a current bug. Blocks enrollment-based tier routing in Round 1 — deferred to Round 3.

 95 + - **L16 Exa broad is the variance bomb.** 25% hit rate but mean 28.6 contacts when it hits, max 58 (Columbus City Schools). When fires, 10-20 extra pages feed into L9 Claude extraction, indirec

 + tly amplifying L9 cost by $0.40-1.20/job. Round 2 candidate for conditional execution only when L9 first pass finds <15 VERIFIED.

 96 +

 97 + ---

 98 +

 104 - 2008 Archdiocese of Philadelphia Schools

 105 - 2009 Archdiocese of Cincinnati Schools

 ⏺ Plan is tight now — feature flags not subclass, real token measurement via module-level capture buffer (thread-safe given ResearchQueue serial guarantee), 20 unit tests, numerical pass/fail gates, defensive
